queries.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM employees LIMIT 100
SELECT * FROM departments LIMIT 100
SELECT * FROM products LIMIT 100
SELECT e.* FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering'
queries.description: Filter by department name with JOIN

queries.natural_language: Find employees with salary greater than 80000
queries.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > 80000
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE is_active = TRUE
SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics'
SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM employees

queries.natural_language: What is the average salary by department
queries.expected_sql: SELECT d.name, AVG(e.salary) as avg_salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.name
SELECT region, SUM(total_amount) as total FROM sales GROUP BY region
SELECT MAX(salary) as max_salary FROM employees
SELECT d.name, COUNT(e.id) as employee_count FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.name
queries.description: COUNT with JOIN and GROUP BY

queries.expected_sql: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 5
SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 10
SELECT * FROM sales ORDER BY sale_date DESC LIMIT 3

queries.natural_language: Show employees with their department names
queries.expected_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, d.name as department FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id

queries.natural_language: List all sales with employee and product details
queries.expected_sql: SELECT s.*, e.first_name, e.last_name, s.product_name FROM sales s JOIN employees e ON s.employee_id = e.id

queries.natural_language: Show projects with their department information
queries.expected_sql: SELECT p.*, d.name as department_name FROM projects p JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.id
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE hire_date >= '2022-01-01' AND hire_date <= '2022-12-31'
SELECT * FROM sales WHERE sale_date >= '2024-01-01' AND sale_date <= '2024-01-31'

queries.natural_language: Average salary of active employees by department, ordered by average salary descending
queries.expected_sql: SELECT d.name, AVG(e.salary) as avg_salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE e.is_active = TRUE GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY avg_salary DESC

queries.natural_language: Total sales amount by employee for employees who made more than 2 sales
queries.expected_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(*) as sale_count, SUM(s.total_amount) as total FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.id = s.employee_id GROUP BY e.id HAVING COUNT(*) > 2

usage_tips: Be specific about table and column names when possible
Use natural language terms like 'greater than', 'less than', 'between'
Specify ordering with words like 'highest', 'lowest', 'most recent', 'oldest'
Use 'by' to indicate grouping (e.g., 'by department', 'by region')
For limits, use 'top N', 'first N', or 'N most/least'
security_notes: Only SELECT queries are allowed by default
Results are limited to prevent excessive data retrieval
SQL injection patterns are detected and blocked
Destructive queries (DROP, DELETE, etc.) require explicit permission
